title: Brothers Little Helper

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashes or has an accident during opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation and scream
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - signature catchphrase perfectly integrated with character, highly quotable

jokes.text: Why, this is retardant. [Laughing] Sure is.
jokes.setup: Skinner explains fire retardant to students
jokes.punchline: Bart treats 'retardant' as an insult/pun
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart wordplay - immature but fits character perfectly

jokes.text: That's called a hose lengthener. [Laughing] You need one.
jokes.punchline: Bart implies Skinner needs a phallic extension
jokes.explanation: Double entendre humor typical of Bart - juvenile but well-executed

jokes.text: Just read the label. It's a king-sized flamer! [Laughing Hysterically]
jokes.punchline: Flamer as both firefighting tool and slur
jokes.explanation: Escalates the pattern but relies on outdated slur - weak by modern standards

jokes.text: Look! A fire... engine. Stop that! Help! Help! Fire... helmets.
jokes.setup: Skinner tries to prevent more inappropriate comments
jokes.punchline: Bart deliberately pauses to create sexual innuendo from innocent words
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation showing Bart's deliberate mischief - well-crafted continuation

jokes.text: Sure, I can do something destructive.
jokes.punchline: Bart mishears/deliberately misinterprets as destructive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - subverts expectation while staying true to character

jokes.text: Just say the word, and I'll drive this hoe in his back! I can make it look like suicide.
jokes.setup: Willie offering to help with problem children
jokes.punchline: Casually offers to murder Bart with gardening tool
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly fitted to Willie's unhinged character - shocking escalation

jokes.text: And I want a bike, and a monkey, and a friend for the monkey-
jokes.punchline: Escalates to wanting a friend for his hypothetical monkey
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation typical of child logic - charming but not exceptional

jokes.text: At my house, we call 'em 'uh-ohs.'
jokes.punchline: Ralph's innocent euphemism for fires/disasters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph line - innocent childlike perspective on serious topic

jokes.text: Tricks? That's rich. He's so inbred, he can barely stand up.
jokes.setup: Child asks if fire dog can do tricks
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of dog's genetic problems
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about animal breeding - unexpected honesty from authority figure

jokes.text: Fire can be our servant... whether it's toasting s'mores or raining down on Charlie.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions napalm/Vietnam War reference
jokes.explanation: Dark juxtaposition of wholesome and wartime uses of fire

jokes.text: What a great pot party! Wasn't it, man?
jokes.setup: Fire safety skit with obvious stoner characters
jokes.punchline: Characters openly reference marijuana use
jokes.explanation: Obvious drug reference in fire safety context - predictable but functional

jokes.text: Maybe we should get another smoke detector in case that one trips out on us. [Chuckling] Why bother, baby? One smoke detector's enough for Mad Dog.
jokes.punchline: Character dismisses fire safety with stoner logic
jokes.explanation: Continues stoner character bit - functional but not inspired

jokes.text: Stop, drop and roll, man! [Chuckling] That's for Clydes, baby. A little fire can't hurt you.
jokes.setup: Fire safety advice given to burning person
jokes.punchline: Character rejects safety advice with cool guy attitude while on fire
jokes.explanation: Absurd commitment to cool guy persona even while burning

jokes.text: The fire burned through the night... and cost him the use of his pants.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of severe burns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement - 'use of his pants' for severe burns is darkly funny

jokes.text: Sorry to break character... but these stunt pants are gettin' pretty toasty! Roll, Neddie! Roll!
jokes.punchline: Actor actually catches fire during fire safety demonstration
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about fire safety demonstration going wrong - ironic and well-executed

jokes.text: It's not workin'! It just spreads the flame!
jokes.punchline: Safety technique makes fire worse
jokes.explanation: Escalates the fire safety failure - good continuation of the bit

jokes.text: What's it doing in the gymnasium? Yee-haw!
jokes.setup: Fire chief asks about hose location
jokes.punchline: Bart has taken the fire hose for joyride
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart chaos - takes advantage of emergency for fun

jokes.text: Yeah. It all kind of came together.
jokes.setup: Bart confronted about his latest destructive act
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual satisfaction with the chaos he caused
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - pride in destruction with casual understatement

jokes.text: Thank you for getting me out of work.
jokes.setup: Called to school about Bart's behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer focuses on benefit to himself rather than concern for son
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer selfishness - perfectly in character

jokes.text: Bart's latest escapade... goes far beyond the realm of the high jink.
jokes.punchline: Pretentious language including made-up word 'high jink'
jokes.explanation: Skinner's pompous vocabulary creates humorous malapropism

jokes.text: There. Now the floor is as good as new. [Groans] My sport goggles!
jokes.setup: Skinner claims to have fixed damage
jokes.punchline: Immediately something else breaks
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick timing - immediate contradiction of claim

jokes.text: Your son is a ravenous demon, relentlessly gnawing at all that's good and true.
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic religious language for school troublemaker
jokes.explanation: Skinner's melodramatic description perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: Yeah, he's something else, all right.
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual agreement misses the severity
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to Skinner's dramatic language

jokes.text: Hey, that looks like Bart.
jokes.setup: Shown statistical pattern of Bart's negative influence
jokes.punchline: Homer focuses on shape resembling Bart rather than data
jokes.explanation: Homer misses point of serious data presentation - classic character move

jokes.text: And turning to the 3-D map, we see an unmistakable cone of ignorance. Put it away! Put it away!
jokes.punchline: Homer can't handle seeing visual representation of his son's impact
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's discomfort with data visualization

jokes.text: You mean like John Leguizamo?
jokes.punchline: Homer's random celebrity reference as his only frame of reference
jokes.explanation: Random celebrity reference typical of Homer's pop culture understanding

jokes.text: Hi, Marge. It's me- Homer. [Clears Throat] What? Time to go?
jokes.setup: Homer gets phone call during serious meeting
jokes.punchline: Homer's obvious lack of awareness about appropriate timing
jokes.explanation: Homer's social obliviousness in serious situation

jokes.text: Gimme an 'F'! [Gasps] Good Lord! Gimme an 'art'! He's gotten into the pep closet.
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately demonstrates by raiding school supplies
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - Bart proves the point being made about him

jokes.text: I'd say he's coming out of the pep closet. [Chuckles]
jokes.punchline: Skinner makes unintentional sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Skinner inadvertently makes suggestive comment - mild wordplay

jokes.text: These are normal guinea pigs running around like idiots. [All Squealing] Now I'll give them some Focusyn.
jokes.setup: Doctor demonstrates drug effects on animals
jokes.punchline: Describes normal animal behavior as 'running around like idiots'
jokes.explanation: Funny perspective on normal animal behavior as problematic

jokes.text: They become your slaves. Yes. But it's not about slavery. It's about helping kids concentrate.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals sinister nature then awkwardly corrects
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about pharmaceutical industry - reveals true nature then backtracks

jokes.text: The only thing more effective is regular exercise. [Shudders]
jokes.punchline: Doctor finds exercise more horrifying than drugging children
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of medical industry preference for pills over lifestyle changes

jokes.text: Some special helpers that'll make you a good boy.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic language for psychiatric drugs
jokes.explanation: Marge's maternal euphemism for powerful drugs

jokes.text: All your favorite stars have used drugs. Brett Butler, Tim Allen- Tommy Lee. Andy Dick. He's just flamboyant. Yeah, and I'm a size four.
jokes.setup: Homer lists celebrity drug users to encourage Bart
jokes.punchline: Marge's sarcastic response to Homer's Andy Dick defense
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - Marge's sarcasm cuts through Homer's naivety

jokes.text: [Gibberish continues]
jokes.setup: Homer ate the drugged candy himself
jokes.punchline: Drug effects making him speak incoherently
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy as Homer suffers consequences of his own trick. Karma moment.

jokes.text: Does Mr. Simpson have a demon, Daddy? Looks like it. Run and get Daddy's exorcism tongs. [Together] Yea!
jokes.punchline: Flanders family has specific tools for demonic possession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Flanders family moment - combines religious extremism with preparation

jokes.text: Yes, I just thought you might love me enough to let me help you.
jokes.setup: Marge promises not to force medication
jokes.punchline: Immediately guilt-trips Bart into taking it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge manipulation - maternal guilt disguised as choice

jokes.text: Way to guilt him, Marge. It's what I do.
jokes.punchline: Marge proudly acknowledges her manipulation technique
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta moment - Marge acknowledges her parenting strategy

jokes.text: I'm having some side effects from the dope. It's not dope! All I know is my testicles won't fit in my underwear.
jokes.punchline: Bart has oranges stuffed in his underwear
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - sets up serious medical concern, reveals innocent explanation

jokes.text: Bart, get those oranges out of there. Back in the lunches you go. Ew, Mom!
jokes.punchline: Marge casually puts them back in lunch preparation
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor typical of family dynamics - Marge's practicality

jokes.text: I'll trade you a Claritin for one. Claritin-D? Nah. Can't help you.
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated drug dealing knowledge among children
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about over-medicated children acting like drug dealers

jokes.text: I take hormones to lower my voice. Now all I wanna do is fight. What are you lookin' at?
jokes.punchline: Hormones make him more aggressive while trying to help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson characterization - medication backfiring in character-appropriate way

jokes.text: You think you got it bad? I gotta wear a shock collar. [Screams] What was that for? I thought about a girl I like.
jokes.punchline: Shocked for having innocent romantic thoughts
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about behavioral conditioning - perfectly innocent Ralph thought punished

jokes.text: Hey, look at those dogs going at it! I wanna see 'em! I wanna see 'em! Fightin' over a fan belt. Dogs are outstanding.
jokes.setup: Students excited about dogs 'going at it' (sexual implication)
jokes.punchline: They're just fighting over car parts, Milhouse's innocent commentary
jokes.explanation: Misdirection with sexual innuendo that turns out innocent. Milhouse's pure appreciation is sweet.

jokes.text: What? You're still in your seat. Well, it's not like I never saw a dog before.
jokes.setup: Distraction causes chaos in classroom except for Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's newfound focus is remarkable to teacher
jokes.explanation: Good reversal showing medication's effect on Bart's behavior

jokes.text: You choked? You choked? Bart! Bart! Krusty just fired his associate producer. I'm reading.
jokes.setup: Lisa excited to tell Bart about TV drama
jokes.punchline: Bart too focused on reading to care about entertainment
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's complete personality change through medication

jokes.text: 'The Seven Habits of Highly Effective Pre-Teens'?
jokes.setup: Lisa sees what Bart is reading
jokes.punchline: Self-help parody book title for children
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of self-help industry targeting children

jokes.text: Joke if you will, but did you know most people use 10% of their brains? I am now one of them.
jokes.setup: Bart cites common brain usage myth
jokes.punchline: Implies he was using even less than 10% before
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that also shows Bart's medicated overconfidence

jokes.text: 'Memo to self: Lock door.' 'Memo to self: Shut up, Lisa.'
jokes.punchline: Escalates to rude dismissal of his sister
jokes.explanation: Shows how medication makes Bart coldly efficient rather than warm

jokes.text: Cost of paper: five cents. A mother's love: priceless.
jokes.setup: Marge reads Bart's thank you note
jokes.punchline: References MasterCard commercial format
jokes.explanation: Simple commercial parody - functional but not original

jokes.text: No, but here's a book called Chicken Soup for the Loser... that gave Bill Buckner the courage to open a chain of laundromats.
jokes.punchline: Parody self-help book with specific failed baseball player reference
jokes.explanation: Good book title parody with specific sports reference

jokes.text: He organized all the lawsuits against me into one class action. That's gonna save all kinds of travel time.
jokes.punchline: Bart organized Homer's legal troubles for efficiency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Bart's efficiency works against his father's interests

jokes.text: I'll ask it how many leagues in a furlong. No, wait. I'll make it say, 'What's a-matter, you?' in Turkish.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about handheld computer capabilities
jokes.punchline: Completely wastes technology on nonsense tasks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - given powerful technology, uses it for silly catchphrases. Very true to character.

jokes.text: A guy could do great things with a gadget like that. Yeah, a guy could do great things.
jokes.punchline: Ominous repetition suggests sinister intentions
jokes.explanation: Builds suspense through repetition - hints at coming plot development

jokes.text: And why did Humpty Dumpty have a great fall? Because he took his eyes off the prize? That's right. You stay on the ball, you stay on the wall.
jokes.punchline: Turns fairy tale into motivational business advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reinterpretation of classic tale as self-help wisdom

jokes.text: He's gone from goofus to gallant. And we owe it all to mind-bending pills.
jokes.punchline: References Highlights magazine and casually mentions 'mind-bending pills'
jokes.explanation: Good reference to children's magazine. 'Mind-bending pills' is darkly honest description.

jokes.text: Ooh! Let's go to the water park! My 10-year ban ended yesterday.
jokes.punchline: Homer misinterprets and reveals past water park incident
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - reveals embarrassing past while missing Marge's hint

jokes.text: Marge, this is the greatest gift... any wife has ever given her husband. I thought you'd like it.
jokes.setup: Homer at strip club, overwhelmed with gratitude
jokes.punchline: Marge casually accepts credit for this romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Funny but not exceptional. Homer's overdramatic gratitude vs Marge's practical response.

jokes.text: Shut up, or I'll pound all of ya! [Yells]
jokes.punchline: Homer threatens violence at inappropriate venue
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate aggression in already inappropriate setting

jokes.text: I heard about a new bar where men dance with men. Doesn't that sound adorable?
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent interpretation of gay bar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge naivety - innocent misunderstanding of adult venue

jokes.text: RayJ. funny, or O.J. funny?
jokes.punchline: Lisa distinguishes between comedian funny and murder suspect funny
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor reference distinguishing types of 'funny behavior'

jokes.text: You're probably wondering about the coat hangers. They're to block the satellite that's been spying on me.
jokes.setup: Bart's room covered in coat hangers
jokes.punchline: Paranoid conspiracy explanation for bizarre decoration
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's medication-induced paranoia through visual comedy

jokes.text: Hey, I had Lenny's name on that! They have it now.
jokes.punchline: Homer worried about trivial organizer information being stolen
jokes.explanation: Homer's focus on mundane details during conspiracy theory

jokes.text: Who else? Major League Baseball.
jokes.setup: Asked who is spying on him
jokes.punchline: Completely random organization as conspiracy villain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd choice - MLB as surveillance villain is completely unexpected and hilarious

jokes.text: Hey, you found my needle-nose pliers.
jokes.setup: Bart threatening to remove dental fillings
jokes.punchline: Homer focused on finding his missing tools
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - more concerned about tools than son's mental state

jokes.text: Oh, that was his idea. He said he felt fat.
jokes.setup: Asked why Bart is on treadmill during medical exam
jokes.punchline: Treats Bart's body image issues casually during psychotic episode
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about normalizing bizarre behavior during medical crisis

jokes.text: He quit blinking. He says that's when they get you.
jokes.punchline: Extreme paranoia about most basic human function
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of paranoia to absurd but believable level

jokes.text: Hmm, and maybe some cyclobenzanone? That's a great idea!
jokes.punchline: Marge enthusiastically adds to dangerous drug mixture
jokes.explanation: Satirizes pharmaceutical industry enthusiasm for drug combinations

jokes.text: No! You can't take my Focusyn. I need every brain cell blazing to outwit my invisible enemies.
jokes.punchline: Bart's addiction and paranoid justification
jokes.explanation: Shows how medication created dependency while feeding paranoia

jokes.text: Uh-oh! Somebody's gonna have a tummy ache.
jokes.setup: Bart consumes dangerous amount of medication
jokes.punchline: Doctor's casual response to potential overdose
jokes.explanation: Dark humor through extreme medical understatement of serious situation

jokes.text: Hey, there was just air in mine.
jokes.setup: Dart gun used to sedate Bart
jokes.punchline: Doctor's weapon misfires harmlessly
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy failure - medical incompetence

jokes.text: [Snickers] Looks like the kid who roughed up the Wilson widow.
jokes.setup: Description of missing boy matches Bart
jokes.punchline: Chief connects to unrelated violent crime
jokes.explanation: Typical Wiggum incompetence - confusing cases inappropriately

jokes.text: Oh, my little guy's out there in the hot sun without his sombrero.
jokes.punchline: Focuses on unusual clothing concern rather than serious danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - maternal worry about oddly specific detail

jokes.text: But how do we know, Marge? How do we know?
jokes.setup: Marge says Bart doesn't have diarrhea
jokes.punchline: Homer's existential crisis about unknowable medical information
jokes.explanation: Homer's philosophical anxiety about trivial medical detail

jokes.text: Smithers, we're at war! I'll begin profiteering, sir. And hoarding.
jokes.punchline: Immediately plans to profit from perceived war
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - immediate war profiteering instinct

jokes.text: Leave it to the Democrats to let the Spaniards back in the pantry.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre political conspiracy theory about Spanish people
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Burns political paranoia - random historical reference

jokes.text: This is a job for the Green Lantern, Thundra... or possibly... Ghost Rider. What about Superman? Oh, please.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy suggests superheroes for crisis
jokes.punchline: Dismisses most popular superhero as inadequate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - snobby dismissal of mainstream hero

jokes.text: Come on! Get out! Noodles, Seesaw, Oopsie... Lobsweat, Hoohah, Jitters, Sir Wide Bottom!
jokes.setup: Bart commanding tank crew to exit
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous made-up military nicknames
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd military nicknames - 'Sir Wide Bottom' is especially funny

jokes.text: You've raised the bar for all of us, Simpson, and I thank you.
jokes.punchline: Officer praises Bart's tank theft as inspirational
jokes.explanation: Ironic praise for destructive behavior - military admiring civilian chaos

jokes.text: Shoot the tires out, Lou. Uh, it's a tank, Chief. You know what? I'm gettin' real tired of your excuses.
jokes.punchline: Frustrated by logical explanation of why it won't work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - angry at reality for not matching his plans

jokes.text: Why don't you just tell everybody?
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive sarcasm about being embarrassed
jokes.explanation: Homer's defensive response when called out. In character but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: All right! Scud the school, dude!
jokes.setup: Bart aims tank at various targets
jokes.punchline: Student enthusiastically supports destroying their own school
jokes.explanation: Standard school destruction wish fulfillment from students

jokes.text: Not the church! Jesus lives there!
jokes.punchline: Ned's childlike religious reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned moment - literal interpretation of religious concepts

jokes.text: The frame store? You monster!
jokes.punchline: Moe's outrage over destruction of mundane business
jokes.explanation: Escalating series of targets - frame store as particularly outrageous

jokes.text: Not the sky! That's where clouds are born!
jokes.punchline: Ralph's innocent misunderstanding of weather/nature
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph line - childlike interpretation creates beautiful absurdity

jokes.text: That's the end of your loony tune, Drugs Bunny.
jokes.punchline: Combines cartoon references with drug reference
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's clumsy wordplay - functional but not brilliant

jokes.text: I told you they were monitoring my activities. He's right! This thing's got info on everybody.
jokes.punchline: Conspiracy turns out to be completely true
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to absurd setup - validates paranoid character completely

jokes.text: Hi, folks. I'm Mark McGwire.
jokes.punchline: Actual celebrity appears to confirm absurd plot
jokes.explanation: Celebrity cameo validates absurd conspiracy - good escalation

jokes.text: Young Bart here was right. We are spying on you pretty much around the clock.
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to massive privacy violation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about privacy violations treated casually by celebrity

jokes.text: Do you wanna know the terrifying truth... or do you wanna see me sock a few dingers? [All] Dingers! Dingers!
jokes.setup: Offered choice between truth and entertainment
jokes.punchline: Everyone chooses entertainment over disturbing reality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of public preferring entertainment to important truths

jokes.text: Plus, you exposed the disturbing unreadiness of today's army.
jokes.setup: Lisa praising Bart's return to normal
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about military incompetence
jokes.explanation: Lisa's intellectual observation about military failure

jokes.text: Why is the zero made out of glass? Who cares? That man can really slug.
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses analysis for simple admiration
jokes.explanation: Homer's anti-intellectual sports worship over suspicious details

jokes.text: Why is the zero made out of glass? Who cares? That man can really slug.
jokes.setup: Bart noticing odd detail in autographed bat
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing obvious forgery concern for hero worship
jokes.explanation: Homer's willful blindness to obvious fake. Good character moment about hero worship.

jokes.text: From now on, it's nothing but fresh air... lots of hugs and good old-fashioned Ritalin.
jokes.punchline: Immediately mentions another psychiatric drug
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending - promises nature then mentions another drug as 'old-fashioned'

jokes.text: When I can't stop fiddlin' I just takes me Ritalin / I'm poppin' and sailin', man
jokes.punchline: Upbeat song about psychiatric medication dependency
jokes.explanation: Musical ending that reinforces the drug theme. Good but not exceptional closer.

jokes.text: Oh, why'd you have to shush? You ruined the whole show!
jokes.punchline: Echoes earlier 'you ruined the whole' complaints from episode
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to recurring phrase - good bookend for episode

tmdb_overview: When Bart commits an extremely destructive prank, he is given Focusyn, a pill to help him concentrate in school. At first the results are impressive, and then Bart starts believing in a conspiracy involving spy satellites and major league baseball.
